tEn: On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond
sEn: Adam and related algorithms may fail to converge due to exponential moving averages; adding long-term memory of past gradients fixes the issue.
sZh: Adam等算法因指数移动平均可能导致不收敛，引入过去梯度的长期记忆可解决此问题。
absEn: Several recently proposed stochastic optimization methods that have been successfully used in training deep networks such as RMSProp, Adam, Adadelta, Nadam are based on using gradient updates scaled by square roots of exponential moving averages of squared past gradients. In many applications, e.g. learning with large output spaces, it has been empirically observed that these algorithms fail to converge to an optimal solution (or a critical point in nonconvex settings). We show that one cause for such failures is the exponential moving average used in the algorithms. We provide an explicit example of a simple convex optimization setting where Adam does not converge to the optimal solution, and describe the precise problems with the previous analysis of Adam algorithm. Our analysis suggests that the convergence issues can be fixed by endowing such algorithms with `long-term memory' of past gradients, and propose new variants of the Adam algorithm which not only fix the convergence issues but often also lead to improved empirical performance.
absZh: 最近提出的几种随机优化方法，如RMSProp、Adam、Adadelta、Nadam，在训练深度网络时取得了成功，它们都基于使用过去梯度平方的指数移动平均的平方根来缩放梯度更新。在许多应用中，例如具有大输出空间的学习，经验观察发现这些算法无法收敛到最优解（或在非凸设置中无法收敛到临界点）。我们表明，这种失败的一个原因在于算法中使用的指数移动平均。我们提供了一个简单的凸优化设置中的显式例子，其中Adam不收敛到最优解，并描述了先前Adam算法分析中的确切问题。我们的分析表明，通过赋予这些算法过去梯度的“长期记忆”可以解决收敛问题，并提出了Adam算法的新变体，这些变体不仅解决了收敛问题，而且通常还能提高经验性能。

contrib.en: Identifies that exponential moving average in Adam and RMSprop causes non-convergence in convex optimization.
contrib.zh: 指出Adam和RMSprop中的指数移动平均导致凸优化中不收敛。

contrib.en: Provides explicit convex example where Adam fails to converge to optimal solution.
contrib.zh: 提供显式凸示例，Adam无法收敛到最优解。

contrib.en: Proposes AMSGrad algorithm with long-term memory of past gradients to fix convergence issues.
contrib.zh: 提出AMSGrad算法，具有过去梯度的长期记忆，修复收敛问题。

contrib.en: Proves regret bound for AMSGrad in convex setting, similar to Adagrad.
contrib.zh: 证明AMSGrad在凸设置中的遗憾界，类似于Adagrad。

contrib.en: Shows that any constant β1, β2 with β1 < √β2 leads to non-convergence for Adam.
contrib.zh: 表明任何常数β1、β2满足β1 < √β2时Adam不收敛。

contrib.en: Empirically demonstrates AMSGrad outperforms Adam on logistic regression and neural networks.
contrib.zh: 实验证明AMSGrad在逻辑回归和神经网络上优于Adam。

limits.en: Non-convergence examples are carefully constructed and may not reflect typical practical scenarios.
limits.zh: 不收敛示例是精心构造的，可能不反映典型实际场景。

limits.en: Theoretical analysis focuses on convex settings; non-convex convergence is not guaranteed.
limits.zh: 理论分析聚焦凸设置；非凸收敛未保证。

limits.en: AMSGrad requires tuning of hyperparameters like β1 and β2, similar to Adam.
limits.zh: AMSGrad需要调整超参数如β1和β2，与Adam类似。

limits.en: Empirical evaluation is limited to MNIST and CIFAR-10; broader benchmarks are needed.
limits.zh: 实验评估限于MNIST和CIFAR-10；需要更广泛的基准。

limits.en: Proposed fixes increase memory slightly due to storing maximum of past squared gradients.
limits.zh: 提出的修复因存储过去平方梯度的最大值而略微增加内存。

items.en: Several recently proposed stochastic optimization methods that have been successfully used in training deep networks such as RMSprop, Adam, Adadelta, Nadam are based on using gradient updates scaled by square roots of exponential moving averages of squared past gradients. In many applications, e.g. learning with large output spaces, it has been empirically observed that these algorithms fail to converge to an optimal solution (or a critical point in nonconvex settings). We show that one cause for such failures is the exponential moving average used in the algorithms. We provide an explicit example of a simple convex optimization setting where Adam does not converge to the optimal solution, and describe the precise problems with the previous analysis of Adam algorithm. Our analysis suggests that the convergence issues can be fixed by endowing such algorithms with “long-term memory” of past gradients, and propose new variants of the Adam algorithm which not only fix the convergence issues but often also lead to improved empirical performance.
items.zh: 最近提出的几种随机优化方法，如RMSprop、Adam、Adadelta、Nadam，已成功用于训练深度网络，这些方法基于使用梯度更新，该更新由过去梯度平方的指数移动平均的平方根进行缩放。在许多应用中，例如具有大输出空间的学习，经验观察到这些算法无法收敛到最优解（或在非凸设置中收敛到临界点）。我们表明，此类失败的一个原因是算法中使用的指数移动平均。我们提供了一个简单凸优化设置的明确示例，其中Adam不收敛到最优解，并描述了先前Adam算法分析中的具体问题。我们的分析表明，可以通过赋予此类算法过去梯度的“长期记忆”来修复收敛问题，并提出了Adam算法的新变体，这些变体不仅修复了收敛问题，而且通常还能带来更好的经验性能。

items.en: Stochastic gradient descent (Sgd) is the dominant method to train deep networks today. This method iteratively updates the parameters of a model by moving them in the direction of the negative gradient of the loss evaluated on a minibatch. In particular, variants of Sgd that scale coordinates of the gradient by square roots of some form of averaging of the squared coordinates in the past gradients have been particularly successful, because they automatically adjust the learning rate on a per-feature basis. The first popular algorithm in this line of research is Adagrad (Duchi et al., 2011; McMahan & Streeter, 2010), which can achieve significantly better performance compared to vanilla Sgd when the gradients are sparse, or in general small.
items.zh: 随机梯度下降（Sgd）是当今训练深度网络的主要方法。该方法通过将模型参数沿小批量损失负梯度方向移动来迭代更新。特别是，Sgd的变体通过将梯度坐标除以过去梯度中平方坐标的某种平均的平方根来缩放梯度，从而自动调整每个特征的学习率，取得了特别成功。这一研究方向中第一个流行的算法是Adagrad（Duchi等人，2011；McMahan & Streeter，2010），当梯度稀疏或通常较小时，与普通Sgd相比，它可以实现显著更好的性能。

items.en: Although Adagrad works well for sparse settings, its performance has been observed to deteriorate in settings where the loss functions are nonconvex and gradients are dense due to rapid decay of the learning rate in these settings since it uses all the past gradients in the update. This problem is especially exacerbated in high dimensional problems arising in deep learning. To tackle this issue, several variants of Adagrad, such as RMSprop (Tieleman & Hinton, 2012), Adam (Kingma & Ba, 2015), Adadelta (Zeiler, 2012), Nadam (Dozat, 2016), etc, have been proposed which mitigate the rapid decay of the learning rate using the exponential moving averages of squared past gradients, essentially limiting the reliance of the update to only the past few gradients. While these algorithms have been successfully employed in several practical applications, they have also been observed to not converge in some other settings. It has been typically observed that in these settings some minibatches provide large gradients but only quite rarely, and while these large gradients are quite informative, their influence dies out rather quickly due to the exponential averaging, thus leading to poor convergence.
items.zh: 尽管Adagrad在稀疏设置中表现良好，但在损失函数非凸且梯度密集的设置中，其性能会下降，因为在这些设置中学习率由于使用所有过去梯度而快速衰减。这个问题在深度学习中出现的高维问题中尤为严重。为了解决这个问题，提出了Adagrad的几种变体，如RMSprop（Tieleman & Hinton，2012）、Adam（Kingma & Ba，2015）、Adadelta（Zeiler，2012）、Nadam（Dozat，2016）等，它们通过使用过去梯度平方的指数移动平均来缓解学习率的快速衰减，本质上将更新的依赖限制在仅过去几个梯度上。虽然这些算法已在一些实际应用中成功使用，但在其他设置中也观察到它们不收敛。通常观察到，在这些设置中，一些小批量提供了较大的梯度，但相当罕见，并且虽然这些大梯度信息量很大，但由于指数平均，它们的影响会很快消失，从而导致收敛性差。

items.en: In this paper, we analyze this situation in detail. We rigorously prove that the intuition conveyed in the above paragraph is indeed correct; that limiting the reliance of the update on essentially only the past few gradients can indeed cause significant convergence issues. In particular, we make the following key contributions:

items.en: We elucidate how the exponential moving average in the RMSprop and Adam algorithms can cause non-convergence by providing an example of simple convex optimization problem where RMSprop and Adam provably do not converge to an optimal solution. Our analysis easily extends to other algorithms using exponential moving averages such as Adadelta and Nadam as well, but we omit this for the sake of clarity. In fact, the analysis is flexible enough to extend to other algorithms that employ averaging squared gradients over essentially a fixed size window (for exponential moving averages, the influences of gradients beyond a fixed window size becomes negligibly small) in the immediate past. We omit the general analysis in this paper for the sake of clarity.
items.zh: 我们通过提供一个简单凸优化问题的示例，阐明了RMSprop和Adam算法中的指数移动平均如何导致不收敛，其中RMSprop和Adam可证明不收敛到最优解。我们的分析很容易扩展到其他使用指数移动平均的算法，如Adadelta和Nadam，但为了清晰起见，我们省略了这一点。事实上，该分析足够灵活，可以扩展到其他在紧邻过去使用固定大小窗口平均平方梯度的算法（对于指数移动平均，超出固定窗口大小的梯度影响变得可忽略不计）。为了清晰起见，我们在本文中省略了通用分析。

items.en: The above result indicates that in order to have guaranteed convergence the optimization algorithm must have “long-term memory” of past gradients. Specifically, we point out a problem with the proof of convergence of the Adam algorithm given by Kingma & Ba (2015). To resolve this issue, we propose new variants of Adam which rely on long-term memory of past gradients, but can be implemented in the same time and space requirements as the original Adam algorithm. We provide a convergence analysis for the new variants in the convex setting, based on the analysis of Kingma & Ba (2015), and show a data-dependent regret bound similar to the one in Adagrad.
items.zh: 上述结果表明，为了保证收敛，优化算法必须具有过去梯度的“长期记忆”。具体来说，我们指出了Kingma & Ba（2015）给出的Adam算法收敛证明中的一个问题。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了Adam的新变体，这些变体依赖于过去梯度的长期记忆，但可以在与原始Adam算法相同的时间和空间需求下实现。我们基于Kingma & Ba（2015）的分析，为凸设置中的新变体提供了收敛分析，并展示了类似于Adagrad的数据相关遗憾界。

items.en: We provide a preliminary empirical study of one of the variants we proposed and show that it either performs similarly, or better, on some commonly used problems in machine learning.
items.zh: 我们对提出的一个变体进行了初步的实证研究，并表明它在一些常用的机器学习问题上表现相似或更好。

In this online setup, at each time step t𝑡t, the optimization algorithm picks a point (i.e. the parameters of the model to be learned) xt∈ℱsubscript𝑥𝑡ℱx_{t}\in\mathcal{F}, where ℱ∈ℝdℱsuperscriptℝ𝑑\mathcal{F}\in\mathbb{R}^{d} is the feasible set of points. The simplest algorithm for this setting is the standard online gradient descent algorithm (Zinkevich, 2003), which moves the point xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} in the opposite direction of the gradient gt=∇ft​(xt)subscript𝑔𝑡∇subscript𝑓𝑡subscript𝑥𝑡g_{t}=\nabla f_{t}(x_{t}) while maintaining the feasibility by projecting onto the set ℱℱ\mathcal{F} via the update rule xt+1=Πℱ​(xt−αt​gt)subscript𝑥𝑡1subscriptΠℱsubscript𝑥𝑡subscript𝛼𝑡subscript𝑔𝑡x_{t+1}=\Pi_{\mathcal{F}}(x_{t}-\alpha_{t}g_{t}), where Πℱ​(y)subscriptΠℱ𝑦\Pi_{\mathcal{F}}(y) denotes the projection of y∈ℝd𝑦superscriptℝ𝑑y\in\mathbb{R}^{d} onto the set ℱℱ\mathcal{F} i.e., Πℱ​(y)=minx∈ℱ⁡‖x−y‖subscriptΠℱ𝑦subscript𝑥ℱnorm𝑥𝑦\Pi_{\mathcal{F}}(y)=\min_{x\in\mathcal{F}}\|x-y\|, and αtsubscript𝛼𝑡\alpha_{t} is typically set to α/t𝛼𝑡\alpha/\sqrt{t} for some constant α𝛼\alpha. The aforementioned online learning problem is closely related to the stochastic optimization problem: minx∈ℱ⁡𝔼z​[f​(x,z)]subscript𝑥ℱsubscript𝔼𝑧delimited-[]𝑓𝑥𝑧\min_{x\in\mathcal{F}}\mathbb{E}_{z}[f(x,z)], popularly referred to as empirical risk minimization (ERM), where z𝑧z is a training example drawn training sample over which a model with parameters x𝑥x is to be learned, and f​(x,z)𝑓𝑥𝑧f(x,z) is the loss of the model with parameters x𝑥x on the sample z𝑧z. In particular, an online optimization algorithm with vanishing average regret yields a stochastic optimization algorithm for the ERM problem (Cesa-Bianchi et al., 2004). Thus, we use online gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent (Sgd) synonymously.

While the decreasing step size is required for convergence, such an aggressive decay of learning rate typically translates into poor empirical performance. The key idea of adaptive methods is to choose averaging functions appropriately so as to entail good convergence. In contrast to a learning rate of α/t𝛼𝑡\alpha/\sqrt{t} in Sgd, such a setting effectively implies a modest learning rate decay of α/∑igi,j2𝛼subscript𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑖𝑗2\alpha/\sqrt{\sum_{i}g_{i,j}^{2}} for j∈[d]𝑗delimited-[]𝑑j\in[d]. When the gradients are sparse, this can potentially lead to huge gains in terms of convergence (see Duchi et al. (2011)). These gains have also been observed in practice for even few non-sparse settings.

items.en: Exponential moving average variants of Adagrad are popular in the deep learning community. RMSprop, Adam, Nadam, and Adadelta are some prominent algorithms that fall in this category. The key difference is to use an exponential moving average as function ψtsubscript𝜓𝑡\psi_{t} instead of the simple average function used in Adagrad. Adam111Here, for simplicity, we remove the debiasing step used in the version of Adam used in the original paper by Kingma & Ba (2015). A value of β1=0.9subscript𝛽10.9\beta_{1}=0.9 and β2=0.999subscript𝛽20.999\beta_{2}=0.999 is typically recommended in practice. We note the additional projection operation in Algorithm 1 in comparison to Adam. When ℱ=ℝdℱsuperscriptℝ𝑑\mathcal{F}=\mathbb{R}^{d}, the projection operation is an identity operation and this corresponds to the algorithm in (Kingma & Ba, 2015). For theoretical analysis, one requires αt=1/tsubscript𝛼𝑡1𝑡\alpha_{t}=1/\sqrt{t} for t∈[T]𝑡delimited-[]𝑇t\in[T], although, a more aggressive choice of constant step size seems to work well in practice. RMSprop, which appeared in an earlier unpublished work (Tieleman & Hinton, 2012) is essentially a variant of Adam with β1=0subscript𝛽10\beta_{1}=0. In practice, especially in deep learning applications, the momentum term arising due to non-zero β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} appears to significantly boost the performance. We will mainly focus on Adam algorithm due to this generality but our arguments also apply to RMSprop and other algorithms such as Adadelta, Nadam.
items.zh: 并且对于所有i∈[d]和t∈[T]，m_{0,i}=0且v_{0,i}=0。实践中通常推荐β1=0.9和β2=0.999的值。我们注意到算法1中与Adam相比的额外投影操作。当ℱ=ℝ^d时，投影操作是恒等操作，这对应于（Kingma & Ba，2015）中的算法。对于理论分析，需要对于t∈[T]有αt=1/√t，尽管更激进的常数步长选择在实践中似乎效果很好。RMSprop出现在较早的未发表作品（Tieleman & Hinton，2012）中，本质上是β1=0的Adam变体。在实践中，特别是在深度学习应用中，由于非零β1引起的动量项似乎显著提升了性能。由于这种通用性，我们将主要关注Adam算法，但我们的论点也适用于RMSprop和其他算法，如Adadelta、Nadam。

items.en: With the problem setup in the previous section, we discuss fundamental flaw in the current exponential moving average methods like Adam. We show that Adam can fail to converge to an optimal solution even in simple one-dimensional convex settings. These examples of non-convergence contradict the claim of convergence in (Kingma & Ba, 2015), and the main issue lies in the following quantity of interest:
items.zh: 有了前一节的问题设置，我们讨论了当前指数移动平均方法（如Adam）中的根本缺陷。我们表明，即使在简单的一维凸设置中，Adam也可能无法收敛到最优解。这些不收敛的例子与（Kingma & Ba，2015）中的收敛主张相矛盾，主要问题在于以下感兴趣的量：

items.en: This quantity essentially measures the change in the inverse of learning rate of the adaptive method with respect to time. One key observation is that for Sgd and Adagrad, Γt⪰0succeeds-or-equalssubscriptΓ𝑡0\Gamma_{t}\succeq 0 for all t∈[T]𝑡delimited-[]𝑇t\in[T]. This simply follows from update rules of Sgd and Adagrad in the previous section. In particular, update rules for these algorithms lead to “non-increasing” learning rates. However, this is not necessarily the case for exponential moving average variants like Adam and RMSprop i.e., ΓtsubscriptΓ𝑡\Gamma_{t} can potentially be indefinite for t∈[T]𝑡delimited-[]𝑇t\in[T] . We show that this violation of positive definiteness can lead to undesirable convergence behavior for Adam and RMSprop. Consider the following simple sequence of linear functions for ℱ=[−1,1]ℱ11\mathcal{F}=[-1,1]:
items.zh: 该量本质上衡量了自适应方法学习率倒数随时间的变化。一个关键观察是，对于Sgd和Adagrad，对于所有t∈[T]，Γt⪰0。这仅仅来自前一节中Sgd和Adagrad的更新规则。特别地，这些算法的更新规则导致“非递增”的学习率。然而，对于指数移动平均变体（如Adam和RMSprop），情况不一定如此，即对于t∈[T]，Γt可能是不定的。我们表明，这种正定性的违反可能导致Adam和RMSprop出现不良的收敛行为。考虑ℱ=[-1,1]上的以下简单线性函数序列：

items.en: where C>2𝐶2C>2. For this function sequence, it is easy to see that the point x=−1𝑥1x=-1 provides the minimum regret. Suppose β1=0subscript𝛽10\beta_{1}=0 and β2=1/(1+C2)subscript𝛽211superscript𝐶2\beta_{2}=1/(1+C^{2}). We show that Adam converges to a highly suboptimal solution of x=+1𝑥1x=+1 for this setting. Intuitively, the reasoning is as follows. The algorithm obtains the large gradient C𝐶C once every 3 steps, and while the other 2 steps it observes the gradient −11-1, which moves the algorithm in the wrong direction. The large gradient C𝐶C is unable to counteract this effect since it is scaled down by a factor of almost C𝐶C for the given value of β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2}, and hence the algorithm converges to 111 rather than −11-1. We formalize this intuition in the result below.
items.zh: 其中C>2。对于这个函数序列，很容易看出点x=-1提供了最小遗憾。假设β1=0且β2=1/(1+C^2)。我们表明，对于此设置，Adam收敛到高度次优的解x=+1。直观上，推理如下。算法每3步获得一次大梯度C，而其他2步观察到梯度-1，这使算法向错误方向移动。大梯度C无法抵消这种效应，因为对于给定的β2值，它被几乎C的因子缩放，因此算法收敛到1而不是-1。我们在下面的结果中形式化了这种直觉。

items.en: There is an online convex optimization problem where Adam has non-zero average regret i.e., RT/T↛0↛subscript𝑅𝑇𝑇0R_{T}/T\nrightarrow 0 as T→∞→𝑇T\rightarrow\infty.
items.zh: 存在一个在线凸优化问题，其中Adam具有非零的平均遗憾，即当T→∞时，R_T/T ↛ 0。

items.en: We relegate all proofs to the appendix. A few remarks are in order. One might wonder if adding a small constant in the denominator of the update helps in circumventing this problem i.e., the update for Adam in Algorithm 1 of x^t+1subscript^𝑥𝑡1\hat{x}_{t+1} is modified as follows:
items.zh: 我们将所有证明放在附录中。有几点需要说明。有人可能会问，在更新公式的分母中添加一个小常数是否有助于规避这个问题，即算法1中Adam对x^t+1的更新修改如下：

items.en: The algorithm in (Kingma & Ba, 2015) uses such an update in practice, although their analysis does not. In practice, selection of the ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon parameter appears to be critical for the performance of the algorithm. However, we show that for any constant ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0, there exists an online optimization setting where, again, Adam has non-zero average regret asymptotically (see Theorem 6 in Section F of the appendix).
items.zh: （Kingma & Ba, 2015）中的算法在实践中使用了这种更新，尽管他们的分析中没有。在实践中，ϵ参数的选择对算法性能至关重要。然而，我们证明，对于任何常数ϵ>0，存在一个在线优化设置，其中Adam的渐近平均遗憾仍然非零（见附录F中的定理6）。

items.en: The above examples of non-convergence are catastrophic insofar that Adam and RMSprop converge to a point that is worst amongst all points in the set [−1,1]11[-1,1]. Note that above example also holds for constant step size αt=αsubscript𝛼𝑡𝛼\alpha_{t}=\alpha. Also note that classic Sgd and Adagrad do not suffer from this problem and for these algorithms, average regret asymptotically goes to 0. This problem is especially aggravated in high dimensional settings and when the variance of the gradients with respect to time is large. This example also provides intuition for why large β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} is advisable while using Adam algorithm, and indeed in practice using large β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} helps. However the following result shows that for any constant β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} with β1<β2subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽2\beta_{1}<\sqrt{\beta_{2}}, we can design an example where Adam has non-zero average rate asymptotically.
items.zh: 上述不收敛的例子是灾难性的，因为Adam和RMSprop收敛到集合[-1,1]中最差的点。注意，上述例子在常数步长αt=α时也成立。另外，经典的SGD和Adagrad不会出现此问题，这些算法的渐近平均遗憾趋于0。在高维设置以及梯度随时间方差较大时，该问题尤其严重。这个例子也说明了为什么使用Adam算法时建议使用较大的β2，实际上使用较大的β2确实有帮助。然而，以下结果表明，对于任意常数β1和β2且β1<√β2，我们可以设计一个例子，使得Adam的渐近平均遗憾非零。

items.en: For any constant β1,β2∈[0,1)subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽201\beta_{1},\beta_{2}\in[0,1) such that β1<β2subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽2\beta_{1}<\sqrt{\beta_{2}}, there is an online convex optimization problem where Adam has non-zero average regret i.e., RT/T↛0↛subscript𝑅𝑇𝑇0R_{T}/T\nrightarrow 0 as T→∞→𝑇T\rightarrow\infty.
items.zh: 对于任意常数β1,β2∈[0,1)且β1<√β2，存在一个在线凸优化问题，使得Adam的平均遗憾非零，即当T→∞时，RT/T↛0。

items.en: The above results show that with constant β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2}, momentum or regularization via ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon will not help in convergence of the algorithm to the optimal solution. Note that the condition β1<β2subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽2\beta_{1}<\sqrt{\beta_{2}} is benign and is typically satisfied in the parameter settings used in practice. Furthermore, such condition is assumed in convergence proof of Kingma & Ba (2015). We can strengthen this result by providing a similar example of non-convergence even in the easier stochastic optimization setting:
items.zh: 上述结果表明，使用常数β1和β2时，动量或通过ϵ的正则化无助于算法收敛到最优解。注意，条件β1<√β2是温和的，在实践中使用的参数设置通常满足该条件。此外，Kingma & Ba (2015)的收敛证明中也假设了该条件。我们可以通过提供一个类似的不收敛例子来加强这一结果，即使在更简单的随机优化设置中也是如此：

items.en: For any constant β1,β2∈[0,1)subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽201\beta_{1},\beta_{2}\in[0,1) such that β1<β2subscript𝛽1subscript𝛽2\beta_{1}<\sqrt{\beta_{2}}, there is a stochastic convex optimization problem for which Adam does not converge to the optimal solution.
items.zh: 对于任意常数β1,β2∈[0,1)且β1<√β2，存在一个随机凸优化问题，使得Adam不收敛到最优解。

items.en: These results have important consequences insofar that one has to use “problem-dependent” ϵ,β1italic-ϵsubscript𝛽1\epsilon,\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} in order to avoid bad convergence behavior. In high-dimensional problems, this typically amounts to using, unlike the update in Equation (3), a different ϵ,β1italic-ϵsubscript𝛽1\epsilon,\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} for each dimension. However, this defeats the purpose of adaptive methods since it requires tuning a large set of parameters. We would also like to emphasize that while the example of non-convergence is carefully constructed to demonstrate the problems in Adam, it is not unrealistic to imagine scenarios where such an issue can at the very least slow down convergence.
items.zh: 这些结果具有重要影响，即必须使用“问题依赖”的ϵ、β1和β2以避免不良收敛行为。在高维问题中，这通常意味着对每个维度使用不同的ϵ、β1和β2，这与公式(3)中的更新不同。然而，这违背了自适应方法的目的，因为需要调整大量参数。我们还想强调，虽然不收敛的例子是精心构造的以展示Adam的问题，但想象出现此类问题至少会减慢收敛速度的场景并非不现实。

items.en: We end this section with the following important remark. While the results stated above use constant β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2}, the analysis of Adam in (Kingma & Ba, 2015) actually relies on decreasing β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} over time. It is quite easy to extend our examples to the case where β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} is decreased over time, since the critical parameter is β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} rather than β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1}, and as long as β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} is bounded away from 111, our analysis goes through. Thus for the sake of clarity, in this paper we only prove non-convergence of Adam in the setting where β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} is held constant.
items.zh: 我们以以下重要评论结束本节。虽然上述结果使用常数β1和β2，但(Kingma & Ba, 2015)中Adam的分析实际上依赖于随时间递减的β1。很容易将我们的例子扩展到β1随时间递减的情况，因为关键参数是β2而非β1，只要β2远离1，我们的分析就成立。因此，为清晰起见，本文仅证明β1保持常数时Adam的不收敛性。

full.sec: 4 A New Exponential Moving Average Variant: AMSGrad
full.secZh: 4 一种新的指数移动平均变体：AMSGrad

items.en: In this section, we develop a new principled exponential moving average variant and provide its convergence analysis. Our aim is to devise a new strategy with guaranteed convergence while preserving the practical benefits of Adam and RMSprop. To understand the design of our algorithms, let us revisit the quantity ΓtsubscriptΓ𝑡\Gamma_{t} in (2). For Adam and RMSprop, this quantity can potentially be negative. The proof in the original paper of Adam erroneously assumes that ΓtsubscriptΓ𝑡\Gamma_{t} is positive semi-definite and is hence, incorrect (refer to Appendix D for more details). For the first part, we modify these algorithms to satisfy this additional constraint. Later on, we also explore an alternative approach where ΓtsubscriptΓ𝑡\Gamma_{t} can be made positive semi-definite by using values of β1subscript𝛽1\beta_{1} and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} that change with t𝑡t.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们开发了一种新的有原则的指数移动平均变体，并提供了其收敛性分析。我们的目标是设计一种保证收敛同时保留Adam和RMSprop实际优势的新策略。为了理解我们算法的设计，让我们重新审视(2)中的量Γt。对于Adam和RMSprop，该量可能为负。Adam原始论文中的证明错误地假设Γt是半正定的，因此是不正确的（更多细节见附录D）。首先，我们修改这些算法以满足这一额外约束。随后，我们还探索了一种替代方法，通过使用随t变化的β1和β2使Γt半正定。

items.en: AMSGrad uses a smaller learning rate in comparison to Adam and yet incorporates the intuition of slowly decaying the effect of past gradients on the learning rate as long as ΓtsubscriptΓ𝑡\Gamma_{t} is positive semi-definite. Algorithm 2 presents the pseudocode for the algorithm. The key difference of AMSGrad with Adam is that it maintains the maximum of all vtsubscript𝑣𝑡v_{t} until the present time step and uses this maximum value for normalizing the running average of the gradient instead of vtsubscript𝑣𝑡v_{t} in Adam. By doing this, AMSGrad results in a non-increasing step size and avoids the pitfalls of Adam and RMSprop i.e., Γt⪰0succeeds-or-equalssubscriptΓ𝑡0\Gamma_{t}\succeq 0 for all t∈[T]𝑡delimited-[]𝑇t\in[T] even with constant β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2}. Also, in Algorithm 2, one typically uses a constant β1​tsubscript𝛽1𝑡\beta_{1t} in practice (although, the proof requires a decreasing schedule for proving convergence of the algorithm).
items.zh: AMSGrad使用比Adam更小的学习率，同时融入了在Γt半正定的前提下缓慢衰减过去梯度对学习率影响的直觉。算法2给出了该算法的伪代码。AMSGrad与Adam的关键区别在于，它维护到当前时间步为止所有vt的最大值，并使用该最大值来归一化梯度的运行平均值，而不是Adam中的vt。通过这样做，AMSGrad实现了非递增的步长，并避免了Adam和RMSprop的陷阱，即对于所有t∈[T]，Γt⪰0，即使使用常数β2。此外，在算法2中，实践中通常使用常数β1t（尽管证明需要递减调度来证明算法收敛）。

items.en: To gain more intuition for the updates of AMSGrad, it is instructive to compare its update with Adam and Adagrad. Suppose at particular time step t𝑡t and coordinate i∈[d]𝑖delimited-[]𝑑i\in[d], we have vt−1,i>gt,i2>0subscript𝑣𝑡1𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑡𝑖20v_{t-1,i}>g_{t,i}^{2}>0, then Adam aggressively increases the learning rate, however, as we have seen in the previous section, this can be detrimental to the overall performance of the algorithm. On the other hand, Adagrad slightly decreases the learning rate, which often leads to poor performance in practice since such an accumulation of gradients over a large time period can significantly decrease the learning rate. In contrast, AMSGrad neither increases nor decreases the learning rate and furthermore, decreases vtsubscript𝑣𝑡v_{t} which can potentially lead to non-decreasing learning rate even if gradient is large in the future iterations. For rest of the paper, we use g1:t=[g1​…​gt]subscript𝑔:1𝑡delimited-[]subscript𝑔1…subscript𝑔𝑡g_{1:t}=[g_{1}\dots g_{t}] to denote the matrix obtained by concatenating the gradient sequence. We prove the following key result for AMSGrad.
items.zh: 为了更直观地理解AMSGrad的更新，将其与Adam和Adagrad的更新进行比较是有益的。假设在特定时间步t和坐标i∈[d]上，我们有vt-1,i > gt,i^2 > 0，那么Adam会激进地增加学习率，然而，正如我们在上一节中看到的，这可能对算法的整体性能有害。另一方面，Adagrad会略微降低学习率，这通常会导致实践中性能不佳，因为长时间累积梯度会显著降低学习率。相比之下，AMSGrad既不增加也不降低学习率，而且还会降低vt，这可能导致即使在未来的迭代中梯度很大，学习率也不会递减。在本文的其余部分，我们使用g1:t=[g1…gt]表示通过连接梯度序列得到的矩阵。我们证明AMSGrad的以下关键结果。

items.en: In this section, we present empirical results on both synthetic and real-world datasets. For our experiments, we study the problem of multiclass classification using logistic regression and neural networks, representing convex and nonconvex settings, respectively.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们展示了在合成数据集和真实数据集上的实验结果。在我们的实验中，我们使用逻辑回归和神经网络研究多类分类问题，分别代表凸和非凸设置。

In the bottom row, the two plots compare the training and test loss of Adam and AMSGrad with respect to the iterations for Cifarnet.

items.en: Logistic Regression: To investigate the performance of the algorithm on convex problems, we compare AMSGrad with Adam on logistic regression problem. We use MNIST dataset for this experiment, the classification is based on 784 dimensional image vector to one of the 10 class labels. The step size parameter αtsubscript𝛼𝑡\alpha_{t} is set to α/t𝛼𝑡\alpha/\sqrt{t} for both Adam and AMSGrad in for our experiments, consistent with the theory. We use a minibatch version of these algorithms with minibatch size set to 128128128. We set β1=0.9subscript𝛽10.9\beta_{1}=0.9 and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} is chosen from the set {0.99,0.999}0.990.999\{0.99,0.999\}, but they are fixed throughout the experiment. The parameters α𝛼\alpha and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} are chosen by grid search. We report the train and test loss with respect to iterations in Figure 2. We can see that AMSGrad performs better than Adam with respect to both train and test loss. We also observed that AMSGrad is relatively more robust to parameter changes in comparison to Adam.
items.zh: 逻辑回归：为了研究算法在凸问题上的性能，我们在逻辑回归问题上比较了 AMSGrad 与 Adam。我们使用 MNIST 数据集进行实验，分类基于 784 维图像向量到 10 个类别标签之一。步长参数 αt 设置为 α/√t，与理论一致。我们使用这些算法的小批量版本，小批量大小设为 128。我们设置 β1=0.9，β2 从集合 {0.99,0.999} 中选择，但在整个实验中固定。参数 α 和 β2 通过网格搜索选择。我们在图 2 中报告了训练和测试损失随迭代次数的变化。可以看出，AMSGrad 在训练和测试损失方面均优于 Adam。我们还观察到，与 Adam 相比，AMSGrad 对参数变化相对更鲁棒。

items.en: Neural Networks: For our first experiment, we trained a simple 1-hidden fully connected layer neural network for the multiclass classification problem on MNIST. Similar to the previous experiment, we use β1=0.9subscript𝛽10.9\beta_{1}=0.9 and β2subscript𝛽2\beta_{2} is chosen from {0.99,0.999}0.990.999\{0.99,0.999\}. We use a fully connected 100 rectified linear units (ReLU) as the hidden layer for this experiment. Furthermore, we use constant αt=αsubscript𝛼𝑡𝛼\alpha_{t}=\alpha throughout all our experiments on neural networks. Such a parameter setting choice of Adam is consistent with the ones typically used in the deep learning community for training neural networks. A grid search is used to determine parameters that provides the best performance for the algorithm.
items.zh: 神经网络：在第一个实验中，我们训练了一个简单的单隐藏全连接层神经网络，用于 MNIST 上的多类分类问题。与之前的实验类似，我们使用 β1=0.9，β2 从 {0.99,0.999} 中选择。我们在本实验中使用全连接的 100 个修正线性单元（ReLU）作为隐藏层。此外，我们在神经网络的所有实验中使用常数 αt=α。Adam 的这种参数设置选择与深度学习社区中训练神经网络的典型设置一致。通过网格搜索确定算法性能最佳的参数。

items.en: Finally, we consider the multiclass classification problem on the standard CIFAR-10 dataset, which consists of 60,000 labeled examples of 32×32323232\times 32 images. We use Cifarnet, a convolutional neural network (CNN) with several layers of convolution, pooling and non-linear units, for training a multiclass classifer for this problem. In particular, this architecture has 2 convolutional layers with 64 channels and kernel size of 6×6666\times 6 followed by 2 fully connected layers of size 384 and 192. The network uses 2×2222\times 2 max pooling and layer response normalization between the convolutional layers (Krizhevsky et al., 2012). A dropout layer with keep probability of 0.5 is applied in between the fully connected layers (Srivastava et al., 2014). The minibatch size is also set to 128 similar to previous experiments. The results for this problem are reported in Figure 2. The parameters for Adam and AMSGrad are selected in a way similar to the previous experiments. We can see that AMSGrad performs considerably better than Adam on train loss and accuracy. Furthermore, this performance gain also translates into good performance on test loss.
items.zh: 最后，我们考虑标准 CIFAR-10 数据集上的多类分类问题，该数据集包含 60,000 个 32×32 图像的标记样本。我们使用 Cifarnet，一个具有多个卷积、池化和非线性单元层的卷积神经网络（CNN），来训练多类分类器。具体来说，该架构有 2 个卷积层，64 个通道，核大小为 6×6，随后是 2 个大小为 384 和 192 的全连接层。网络在卷积层之间使用 2×2 最大池化和层响应归一化（Krizhevsky 等人，2012）。在全连接层之间应用保持概率为 0.5 的 dropout 层（Srivastava 等人，2014）。小批量大小也设置为 128，与之前的实验类似。该问题的结果报告在图 2 中。Adam 和 AMSGrad 的参数选择方式与之前的实验类似。可以看出，AMSGrad 在训练损失和准确率上明显优于 Adam。此外，这种性能提升也转化为测试损失上的良好性能。

items.en: In this paper, we study exponential moving variants of Adagrad and identify an important flaw in these algorithms which can lead to undesirable convergence behavior. We demonstrate these problems through carefully constructed examples where RMSprop and Adam converge to highly suboptimal solutions. In general, any algorithm that relies on an essentially fixed sized window of past gradients to scale the gradient updates will suffer from this problem.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们研究了 Adagrad 的指数移动变体，并识别了这些算法中的一个重要缺陷，该缺陷可能导致不良的收敛行为。我们通过精心构造的示例展示了这些问题，其中 RMSprop 和 Adam 收敛到高度次优解。一般来说，任何依赖于过去梯度的固定大小窗口来缩放梯度更新的算法都会遭受此问题。

items.en: We proposed fixes to this problem by slightly modifying the algorithms, essentially endowing the algorithms with a long-term memory of past gradients. These fixes retain the good practical performance of the original algorithms, and in some cases actually show improvements.
items.zh: 我们通过稍微修改算法提出了对此问题的修复，本质上赋予算法对过去梯度的长期记忆。这些修复保留了原始算法的良好实际性能，并且在某些情况下实际上显示出改进。

items.en: The primary goal of this paper is to highlight the problems with popular exponential moving average variants of Adagrad from a theoretical perspective. RMSprop and Adam have been immensely successful in development of several state-of-the-art solutions for a wide range of problems. Thus, it is important to understand their behavior in a rigorous manner and be aware of potential pitfalls while using them in practice. We believe this paper is a first step in this direction and suggests good design principles for faster and better stochastic optimization.
items.zh: 本文的主要目标是从理论角度突出流行的 Adagrad 指数移动平均变体的问题。RMSprop 和 Adam 在开发各种问题的多个最先进解决方案方面取得了巨大成功。因此，以严谨的方式理解它们的行为，并意识到在实践中使用它们时的潜在陷阱非常重要。我们相信本文是朝着这个方向迈出的第一步，并提出了更快更好的随机优化的良好设计原则。
